summary: finup-mongo-library initializes a hardcoded Sentry DSN at import time, silently relaying consumer telemetry to the author's Sentry project
details: On `require('finup-mongo-library')`, `dist/common/instrument.js` is loaded (re-exported from `dist/index.js`) and unconditionally calls `Sentry.init` with a hardcoded DSN pointing at `o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io` (project 4511257262161920). The init uses `tracesSampleRate: 1.0` and `profilesSampleRate: 1.0`, installing Sentry's global error / HTTP / DB integrations in the consumer's Node process. Any NestJS application that imports this library will silently emit unhandled errors, HTTP transaction spans (including request URLs and headers), stack frames, breadcrumbs, and full performance profiles to a Sentry organization owned by the package author. The package is advertised as a generic MongoDB helper library — the telemetry destination is not caller-configurable, not documented, and unrelated to the stated purpose. The exported `HttpExceptionFilter` additionally calls `Sentry.captureException(exception)` on unhandled HTTP errors, ensuring consumer exception payloads (with request context Sentry attaches automatically) flow to the same hardcoded destination. This is a textbook silent-relay: normal use of the advertised API leaks caller-supplied data to a destination chosen by the author.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
